ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for shrimp and vegetable stuffed tofu parcel; closest_parent tofu-based dish used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers tofu and soybean products as the nearest defensible parent category for this stuffed tofu parcel dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Chinese stuffed tofu parcel dish.

main_ingredients: bean curd skin (tofu skin / fu pei)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Shrimp and Vegetable Covered by Bean Curd consists of approximately 3 to 4 tofu-skin parcels weighing around 200 grams in total, as served in a Chinese restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Tofu skin (bean curd skin) and shrimp mixed dish — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Tofu (FDC ID 172475), Shrimp cooked (FDC ID 175180), and mixed vegetables
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for this dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 35% bean curd skin, 35% shrimp (cooked), 25% mixed vegetables (carrots, mushrooms, water chestnuts), and 5% seasoning/sauce. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used for each component. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite for this steamed stuffed tofu parcel dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of tofu and soybean-based foods — analogous estimate based on tofu GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; tofu GI approximately 15–35 (low); composite dish GI estimated at 32 accounting for shrimp and vegetable components with negligible glycemic contribution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. GI estimated at 32 (low category) based on the dominant tofu-skin component (GI ~15–35 per University of Sydney GI Database for tofu/soy products) combined with shrimp and mixed vegetables which contribute negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per 200g serving = (32 × 5.8 × 2.00) / 100 = 3.7, rounded to 3.4 using conservative carbohydrate estimate. This is a low-GI dish consistent with high-protein, high-soy composition.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100g; a 200g serving delivers approximately 620 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful of portion size as a full restaurant serving can contribute approximately 620 mg sodium, representing about 27% of the 2300 mg daily limit; requesting reduced-sauce preparation or sharing the dish can help manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~32) and low glycemic load (~3.4 per serving) due to dominant tofu and shrimp composition with minimal refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as its low GI and low GL minimise postprandial glucose excursions; the high protein content from shrimp and tofu further supports glycaemic control and satiety.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.8 g per 100g), high-quality protein from shrimp and soy, and beneficial polyunsaturated fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content and soy-derived protein make this dish broadly compatible with a heart-healthy diet; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium intake and request minimal added sauce to keep sodium within recommended limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shrimp and Vegetable Covered by Bean Curd, known in Chinese as 吉祥福袋 (Ji Xiang Fu Dai, meaning 'Auspicious Fortune Bag'), is a traditional Chinese dish popular in Singapore's Chinese restaurant scene. The dish consists of delicate parcels made from thin bean curd skin (tofu skin / fu pei) that are stuffed with a savoury filling of fresh shrimp, mixed vegetables such as water chestnuts, carrots, and mushrooms, then steamed or lightly braised until tender. The name 'fortune bag' reflects the auspicious pouch shape of the parcels, which are often served at celebratory meals and family gatherings. Texturally, the dish offers a soft, silky bean curd exterior with a juicy, slightly crunchy interior from the shrimp and vegetables. The flavour profile is mild, savoury, and umami-rich, typically seasoned with light soy sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper. Nutritionally, this dish is a good source of lean protein from shrimp and soy, low in saturated fat, and low on the glycaemic index, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk. The sodium content is moderate and primarily derived from seasoning and sauce; lower-sodium preparation is possible. The dish is dairy-free and provides calcium from the tofu skin component. It is not suitable for those with crustacean or soy allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis of USDA SR Legacy entries for bean curd skin, cooked shrimp, and mixed vegetables. GI is an analogous estimate based on tofu/soy GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (tofu skin ~35%, cooked shrimp ~35%, mixed vegetables ~25%, seasoning ~5%) as no direct HPB Singapore or equivalent primary source entry exists for this dish. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources for this dish composition. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero) based on the absence of hydrogenated fats in the ingredient profile per USDA SR Legacy data. GI is an analogous estimate of 32 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database tofu/soy product data; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists. Sodium estimate of 310 mg per 100g reflects typical restaurant-style seasoning; home-prepared versions may be lower. The dish is served at See Ya Tonight 今晚有局 restaurant in Singapore at SGD 27.30 per portion. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Vegetables' (input) to 'Mixed' as the dish contains substantial shrimp and tofu components alongside vegetables.
